import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/opt/triposr')

import os
import json
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
import math
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image
from tsr.system import TSR
import inspect

device = 'cuda:0'

# Load model
model = TSR.from_pretrained(
    "/models/triposr",
    config_name="config.yaml",
    weight_name="model.ckpt",
)
model.renderer.set_chunk_size(131072)
model.to(device)
model.eval()

# Check get_spherical_cameras source
from tsr.utils import get_spherical_cameras
print("=== get_spherical_cameras source ===")
print(inspect.getsource(get_spherical_cameras))

# Check model.render source
print("\n=== model.render source ===")
print(inspect.getsource(model.render))

# Load a validation object
data_dir = os.environ.get('RAB_DATA_DIR', '/data')
val_dir = os.path.join(data_dir, 'val')
objects = sorted(os.listdir(val_dir))[:1]
uid = objects[0]
obj_dir = os.path.join(val_dir, uid)

with open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'cameras.json')) as f:
    cameras = json.load(f)

print(f"\n=== Camera params for {uid} ===")
for cam in cameras:
    print(f"  View {cam['view_id']}: az={cam['azimuth']:.1f}, el={cam['elevation']:.1f}, dist={cam['distance']:.3f}")

# Load input and run TripoSR
img = Image.open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'input.png'))
if img.mode == 'RGBA':
    background = Image.new('RGB', img.size, (255, 255, 255))
    background.paste(img, mask=img.split()[3])
    img_rgb = background
else:
    img_rgb = img.convert('RGB')

with torch.no_grad():
    scene_codes = model([img_rgb], device=device)

print(f"\nScene codes shape: {scene_codes.shape}")

# Use built-in render to get reference images
print("\n=== Built-in render ===")
with torch.no_grad():
    # Default render: n_views=30, elevation_deg=0, camera_distance=1.9
    frames = model.render(scene_codes, n_views=4, return_type='pil')
    print(f"Frames type: {type(frames)}, len: {len(frames)}")
    if isinstance(frames, list):
        for i, f in enumerate(frames):
            if isinstance(f, Image.Image):
                print(f"  Frame {i}: {f.size}, mode={f.mode}")
                f.save(f'/tmp/builtin_render_{i}.png')
            elif isinstance(f, dict):
                for k, v in f.items():
                    if isinstance(v, Image.Image):
                        print(f"  Frame {i} {k}: {v.size}, mode={v.mode}")
                        v.save(f'/tmp/builtin_render_{i}_{k}.png')
                    elif isinstance(v, list):
                        print(f"  Frame {i} {k}: list of {len(v)}")
                        for j, item in enumerate(v[:2]):
                            if isinstance(item, Image.Image):
                                print(f"    {j}: {item.size}, mode={item.mode}")
                                item.save(f'/tmp/builtin_render_{i}_{k}_{j}.png')

# Now try rendering with get_spherical_cameras at specific poses
print("\n=== Custom render at target poses ===")
cam = cameras[1]  # First target view
print(f"Target: az={cam['azimuth']}, el={cam['elevation']}, dist={cam['distance']}")

# Check what get_spherical_cameras returns
test_cams = get_spherical_cameras(
    n_views=1,
    elevation_deg=cam['elevation'],
    camera_distance=cam['distance'],
)
print(f"get_spherical_cameras output type: {type(test_cams)}")
if isinstance(test_cams, torch.Tensor):
    print(f"  shape: {test_cams.shape}")
    print(f"  values:\n{test_cams}")

# Also check with multiple views to see azimuth convention
test_cams4 = get_spherical_cameras(
    n_views=4,
    elevation_deg=0,
    camera_distance=1.9,
)
print(f"\n4-view cameras shape: {test_cams4.shape}")
for i in range(4):
    print(f"  View {i}: {test_cams4[i]}")
